title: conyza pallidiflora
intro: Conyza pallidiflora, also known as pale fleabane, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) characterized by its pale yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. Conyza pallidiflora is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic to prevent wound infections. The plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and its leaves and flowers can be used in herbal preparations, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
